regimeSub: Risk-on equity melt-up colliding with sticky 4.2% CPI and a Warsh Fed that dropped its cutting bias and is flirting with a hike.
themes: Iran-US ceasefire deal caps oil near $73 -> energy disinflation relief but headline CPI still 4.2%
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias, hysteresis talk points to September HIKE -> front-end stays pinned
AI/energy capex boom + de-escalation -> Dow above 52k, SPX 7440, global risk-on broadens
Negative real policy rate + soft DXY (101) -> gold rockets to ~$4000 as debasement/credibility hedge

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real; Fed behind curve, hike risk live despite cooling oil.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp (4.07/4.38); modest steepening but flat-ish as hike fears anchor the front end.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17.6 and record equities signal no credit stress, complacent risk appetite.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65 — calm post-ceasefire, but underprices a hawkish Warsh and sticky-inflation tail.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73.5, deflated by US-Iran deal halting attacks; supply-surge pricing eases inflation impulse.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: ~$3993 near records — feeding on negative real rates, Fed independence fight (Cook case), debasement.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1547 weak won despite KOSPI 8557 melt-up; yen at 162 carry pressure spilling across Asia FX.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/energy capex supercycle driving leadership; Tesla, Alphabet, Rocket Lab gains confirm tech bid.

headline: Equities are at records on an Iran-US ceasefire and AI capex euphoria, but inflation at a 3-year-high 4.2% and a hawkish Warsh Fed threatening a September hike leave the rally riding negative real rates and record gold.
